content.full_text:
Law Offices of

OLSON, CONNELLY, HAGEL, FONG & LEIDIGH

January 27, 1992

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 600
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Scott:

This letter is sent to follow up on your telephone
conversation with Lance. He asked for your written
response to the question whether Government Code
section 85201(a) applied to a candidate-controlled
committee which was formed prior to January 1, 1989 and
which did not solicit or receive any contributions
after January 1, 1989.

Specifically our question is whether Section
85201(a) and the corresponding Regulation 18524
prohibits such a committee from placing part of its
funds in an investment account with an out-of-state
brokerage company.

Government Code sections 85200 and 85201 were part
of Proposition 73, which took effect January 1, 1989.
Section 85200 requires a candidate to file a statement
of intention (FPPC Form 501) prior to soliciting or
receiving campaign contributions. After the filing of
the statement of intention, section 85201 requires the
candidate to establish a campaign bank account at an
office of a financial institution located in the state,
and to file specified information concerning the bank
account with the FPPC (FPPC Form 502).

The Interim Information Manual on Propositions 68
and 73 issued by the FPPC in March 1989 (p. 23) states
that Forms 501 and 502 are "required to be filed only
for contributions solicited or received after January
1, 1989." It is our understanding that, for committees
which did not solicit or receive any contributions
after January 1, 1989, the requirements of sections
85200 and 85201 do not apply.

Accordingly, such a committee would not have to
file Forms 501 and 502 and would not be governed by any
of the other requirements of sections 85200 and 85201.
Since the prohibition on placing committee funds in an
out-of-state brokerage company is based on section
85201, this specific requirement would also not apply.

LANCE H. OLSON
BRUCE J. HAGEL
LEROY V. FONG
ROBERT E. LEIDIGH
GEORGE M. WATERS
DIANE M. FISHBURN

OF COUNSEL
LLOYD C. CONNELLY, Member
California State Legislature

300 Capitol Mall, Suite 350
Sacramento, California 95814

TELEPHONE: (916) 442-2952
FAX: (916) 442-1280

Scott Hallabrin
January 27, 1992
Page 2


     I understand that you stated that, while you generally
agreed with our analysis of this issue, you would like to consult
with others on this issue before providing written confirmation.
If you would like to discuss this further, please call me or
Lance.

     We appreciate your expedited response to our request.

Sincerely,

OLSON, CONNELLY, HAGEL,
FONG & LEIDIGH

[signature]

DIANE M. FISHBURN
DMF/ccz

sections.question: Whether Government Code section 85201(a) and the corresponding Regulation 18524 prohibits a candidate-controlled committee, which was formed prior to January 1, 1989 and which did not solicit or receive any contributions after January 1, 1989, from placing part of its funds in an investment account with an out-of-state brokerage company.
sections.question_synthetic: The question asks whether Government Code section 85201(a) applies to a pre-1989 candidate-controlled committee that has not solicited or received contributions since January 1, 1989, and specifically whether such a committee may place funds in an out-of-state brokerage account.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from an attorney, not an FPPC response letter. The letter references a prior telephone conversation and requests written confirmation on an analysis of Proposition 73 requirements. No formal CONCLUSION or ADVICE section is present as this is requesting FPPC's response, not providing it.

embedding.summary: This is an incoming letter from the law firm Olson, Connelly, Hagel, Fong & Leidigh to FPPC Acting General Counsel Scott Hallabrin requesting written confirmation regarding the application of Government Code section 85201(a) to pre-1989 candidate-controlled committees that have not solicited contributions since January 1, 1989, specifically regarding investment of funds with out-of-state brokers.
